headline: Chess: Carlsen competes on Wall Street as US players target world crowns
body_text:
Magnus Carlsen, the global No 1, comes to Wall Street this week as the Norwegian legend, 34, defends his World Rapid and Blitz crowns in New York against a host of ambitious US challengers.

This will be the first time that the two popular speed world championships have been staged on American soil. Rapid is defined as 15 minutes per player per game, plus an increment of 10 seconds a move from move one, while Blitz is three minutes per player per game, plus a two-seconds-per-move increment.

Carlsen has already won five world rapids and seven world blitzes in his illustrious career. Rapid has become his favourite format, and he has just provided another example in last week’s Champions Chess Tour, where most of the events were held online, with all grandmasters eligible to enter, but the final group of eight was staged across the board in Oslo.

The complex format ended up with a best-of-six-games final between Carlsen and his old rival Ian Nepomniachtchi, whom he memorably defeated in
